programmes.show_description: The Entertainer: Series two starts with Fran, ever keen to distract herself, taking up piano. Ever keen to delude himself, Bernard falls in love. All Manny wants is a break.
Fever: Fran can't sleep in the heat. Bernard needs a girlfriend to stop him staring. And Manny can't say what will happen if the temperature gets to 88 degrees.
Compilation show of the comedy panel game from 2012, with host Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and an array of celebrity guests.
Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson return in the comedy panel game. Guests in this first show are Joe Wilkinson, Ellie Taylor, Natalie Cassidy and Gabby Logan.
The Entertainer: Series two starts with Fran, ever keen to distract herself, taking up piano. Ever keen to delude himself, Bernard falls in love. All Manny wants is a break.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
The Hot Tub One: While bride-to-be Hayley always dreamed of a traditional Jewish celebration, bodylicious Ben would prefer a hot tub wedding. Will Hayley take the plunge?
The One with All the Pixies: Sam has £13k to spend on his pixie wedding to his love, Luanne. Will Luanne, who dreams of a traditional service, appreciate his scheme?
The One with the Dinosaurs: Elle has her heart set on a classy wedding at Liverpool's iconic Liver Building. But Phil plans a big day to go down in history.
Lorne Abony, the CEO and chairman of in-store media provider Mood Media, one of the fastest-growing companies in America, adopts a disguise to go undercover in his own business.
Jim Rogers, the chairman and CEO of Kampgrounds of America, the largest campsite organisation in the world, goes undercover in his business, disguised as an accountant.
There are bad table manners, disagreements about gender roles and spoonfuls of criticism - oh, and some British, European and Asian food too - in this episode from Somerset.
There are Thai classics, revelations, dancing and problematic experimental desserts as three couples compete in and around Bristol.
In East Anglia, Paul and Jill plan an Indian buffet inspired by their trips abroad. Lovebirds Tom and Bonnie serve a breakfast-themed bonanza. And gym bunnies Neil and Emma get competitive.
In East Anglia, Nikki and Clive host their first-ever dinner party, Lloyd and Molly serve up Thai treats, and Pete and Kirsty host with a letter P theme - but is it perfect or painful?
Peter's: Gordon visits Peter's Italian Restaurant in New York, where the peace of Long Island is shattered by the antics of the volatile Pellegrino family who run the place.
The Mixing Bowl: Bijou New York diner The Mixing Bowl is haemorrhaging money thanks to manager Mike's out-of-control marketing plans. Can Gordon get things back on track?
Seascape: Gordon Ramsay visits the Seascape restaurant in the seaside village of Islip, on Long Island in New York, where owner Peter has nearly a million dollars of debt.
The Secret Garden: Gordon Ramsay visits a romantic French restaurant in California, where temperamental chef Michel Bardavid's complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away.
Lela's: In California, Gordon Ramsay visits Lela's, a fine dining restaurant run by inexperienced owner Lela and young chef Ricky. Can Gordon save them from complete disaster?
Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host. Jon Richardson and Roisin Conaty take on Rob Beckett and Kiell Smith-Bynoe. Lou Wall joins Susie in Dictionary Corner.
The funniest bearded, babbling, bug-eyed druid the world has ever seen, captured in his natural habitat: the Hammersmith Apollo, during his sold-out comedy tour.
The Fixer: Manny uses his underworld connections to find Fran mysterious employment.  In return Bernard and Manny just have to educate a psychopath.
Blood: In pursuit of Michelin stars, Manny and Bernard transform the infested bookshop into a restaurant. Meanwhile, Fran explores her family tree.
Best bits from the last series of 8 Out of 10 Cats, with Jimmy Carr, Rob Beckett, Katherine Ryan, Tom Allen, Rosie Jones, Phil Wang, Gemma Collins, Richard Osman and many more.
An extended edition of the panel show with host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, plus guests Hannibal Buress, Sam Buttery, Stephen Mangan and Katherine Ryan.
An extended edition with host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, who are joined by guests David Hasselhoff, Rachel Riley, Rob Beckett and Justin Moorhouse.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Anthony Wedo, the president and CEO of buffet restaurant organisation Buffets, Inc., goes undercover among his workforce. Can he turn the firm around after years of challenges?
Amit Kleinberger, CEO of frozen yogurt chain Menchie's, goes undercover to work out what changes must be made at grassroots level to make the company a billion-dollar business.
In Luton, there's unpalatable behaviour, curdled ice cream, an eggy mishap and a hair-raising confession, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
In Devon, Matt and Sarah admit to not knowing what scallops or celeriac are, Jake and Ian question the canapés (crisps), and foodies James and Laura get competitive with a French theme.
...Selling: Francis, Juanita and their kids still love their home but they've outgrown it. Can Jonathan make it look brand-spanking new while Drew searches for a high-class house?
The One with the Helicopter: Reece, who is ex-military, plans a wedding with an army twist. But his bride Lucy would prefer something in a castle.
The Adventure Sports One: Excitable groom Jonny plans an adrenaline-fuelled wedding for his bride-to-be Kate, who favours something simple.
Undercover bosses Amit Kleinberger, Randy DeWitt and Steve Greenbaum take another look inside their firms, but this time they each send an employee undercover in their place.
Jeff Platt, the president and CEO of a chain of indoor trampoline parks, goes undercover in his own company, to find out whether his fast-growing business is running smoothly.
Lakeview Hotel: Gordon Ramsay confronts the owner of the Lakeview Hotel on Lake Chelan in Washington. The bedrooms are empty and the restaurant and ice cream parlour are failing.
Brick Hotel: Can Gordon convince the owner of the historic Brick Hotel in Newtown, Pennsylvania, to value her loyal workers and entice locals back to this landmark establishment?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Mike, Steve and their four children have outgrown their cosy cottage. Will a 200-year-old property be what they're looking for?
Much Ado About Crushing: The fight for Chase's affection heats up. And the drama bubbles over as a wild night out in a hot tub ends with an unexpected bang.
The contestants argue about facial hair. Mike Wozniak vaults a high fence. And Lee Mack struggles to see a balloon.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Miles Jupp take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo. Vic Reeves joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Victoria Coren Mitchell take on Jon Richardson and James Acaster. Morgana Robinson joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jeremy Makes It: Jeremy meets an old classmate, Gog, who he used to bully. Gog is now successful and invites Jeremy to work on his latest art house movie.
Local Zero: Jeff is going on a work trip with Sophie to Aberdeen, and Mark - fearful they will sleep together - becomes desperate to stop them...
